subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the growth of Indian nationalism during 1919–1930s
Identify key events like Jallianwala Bagh, Non-Cooperation Movement, and Civil Disobedience Movement
Recognize contributions of leaders such as Mahatma Gandhi, Nehru, and Sardar Patel
Learn about the role of peasants, workers, and women in nationalist movements
Develop ability to relate historical movements to modern India’s freedom struggle

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Indian Nationalism Post-1919
topics_covered: Overview of political scenario in India after World War I
Impact of the Rowlatt Act and Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
Rise of mass movements under Mahatma Gandhi
teaching_strategy: Show timeline of key events post-1919
Discuss significance of Jallianwala Bagh in simple language
student_activities: Locate Punjab and other key regions on map
Oral discussion on the impact of Jallianwala Bagh
assessment: Ask students to explain how Jallianwala Bagh influenced nationalism
Quick oral Q&A on Rowlatt Act

lesson_plan.title: Non-Cooperation Movement
topics_covered: Objectives and causes of the Non-Cooperation Movement
Role of Mahatma Gandhi, Congress leaders, and masses
Methods: boycott, protests, and swadeshi activities
teaching_strategy: Show images of protests and leaders
Discuss movement in a simple story format
student_activities: Match leaders with their roles in the movement
Identify key regions of mass participation on the map
assessment: Ask students to name one method and one leader involved in Non-Cooperation Movement

lesson_plan.title: Civil Disobedience Movement – Part 1
topics_covered: Salt March of 1930 and its significance
Mass participation of peasants and workers
Government repression and arrests
teaching_strategy: Storytelling approach for Salt March and protests
Show maps and images of participants
student_activities: Create a simple timeline of Civil Disobedience Movement events
assessment: Short oral quiz on Salt March and its impact

lesson_plan.title: Civil Disobedience Movement – Part 2
topics_covered: Boycotts of foreign goods and schools
Participation of women and youth
Regional protests and movements across India
teaching_strategy: Use maps to show regional activities
Discussion on how women and youth contributed
student_activities: Identify regions where major protests occurred
List ways in which women participated in the movement
assessment: Ask students to explain the significance of regional protests
Quick oral Q&A on participation of different groups

lesson_plan.title: Peasant and Worker Movements
topics_covered: Peasant uprisings in Bengal, Punjab, and other regions
Workers’ strikes and role of trade unions
Impact on nationalist movement and awareness
teaching_strategy: Show images of peasant and worker protests
Discuss the connection between economic grievances and nationalism
student_activities: Map major peasant and worker movements
Class discussion on causes and outcomes of protests
assessment: Oral questions on regions and leaders of peasant movements
Short written answer on worker strikes and impact

lesson_plan.title: Role of Women in Nationalist Movements
topics_covered: Women leaders and activists: Sarojini Naidu, Kasturba Gandhi, Annie Besant
Participation in protests, picketing, and public meetings
Challenges faced by women activists
teaching_strategy: Show images and biographies of women activists
Discuss importance of women’s role in simple terms
student_activities: Identify women participants on timeline and map
Explain one contribution of each activist in one sentence
assessment: Short oral quiz on women activists
Class discussion on lessons learned from their participation

lesson_plan.title: Chapter Revision and Synthesis
topics_covered: Summary of key events: Rowlatt Act, Jallianwala Bagh, Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience
Leaders, movements, and mass participation
Review of chapter through maps, timelines, and leader cards
teaching_strategy: Revision using interactive activities: map labeling, leader flashcards
Class discussion linking all movements and their impact
student_activities: Group quiz on chapter events, leaders, and participation
Fill timeline and map activities for review
assessment: Short oral quiz covering all events and leaders
Class discussion to summarize lessons learned

teaching_aids: Maps of India showing major protests and movements
Images of leaders, women activists, and mass movements
Flashcards with leaders, movements, and contributions
teacher_tip: Use visuals, storytelling, and maps for easy-level students. Focus on major movements, key leaders, and participation of different groups. Encourage students to actively participate by identifying leaders and movements on maps and timelines.
